plan.executiveSummary: Advisory recommendation: advance STR-BALANCED to human decision gates. It is the only modeled strategy compatible with the Day 14 and Day 21 targets and $420,000 cap. The projection is not an observation; execution remains prohibited pending the Action Contract, required approvals, and operational confirmations.
plan.strategyRationale: Verified facts show a 12-day delay and only 5.4 usable coverage days against $8.7 million exposed revenue. Current protection remains 0% observed. The STR-BALANCED simulation projects 93.2% protection by Day 14 and 96.4% by Day 21 at an estimated $386,000, leaving $34,000 headroom. STR-SPEED breaches the budget, while STR-COST misses both targets. Balanced remains conditional because its 94.7% downside case would miss the final target.
evidenceCitations: Supplier, carrier, and purchase-order records confirm the 12-day shipment delay (EVD-EDI-101, EVD-CAR-102, and EVD-PO-104).
Restricted inventory deductions reproduce 5.4 usable production days (EVD-CALC-111).
NX-400 requires MAT-C17 without an unrestricted substitute (EVD-BOM-108).
The alternate supplier is conditionally approved and requires fresh batch validation (EVD-QLT-115).
The strategy costs, protection projections, confidence levels, and downside cases are simulations in evidencePacket.strategies.
Targets, constraints, approval requirements, and the observed 0% current state are defined in evidencePacket.outcomeContract.
Dependencies, checkpoints, draft-only communications, and pause-and-re-escalate guards are defined in evidencePacket.proposedActionGraph.

assumptions.statement: Quality release completes within 36 hours; validation is required, but completion and timing are not observed.
The interplant transfer clears customs; validation and contingency controls are proposed, but clearance is not confirmed.
Weekend labor is available; confirmation remains pending.
Compressor supply does not reduce protection below 95%; the packet identifies this as a downside risk.

crossTeamPriorities.priority: Maintain the $420,000 ceiling and verify authority for the $386,000 estimate.
crossTeamPriorities.dependency: Plant COO and CFO-delegate approval plus a human-approved Action Contract.

crossTeamPriorities.priority: Establish alternate capacity and preserve the approved-or-conditionally-approved supplier restriction.
crossTeamPriorities.dependency: Quality release, Finance authority, and Global Procurement VP approval before commitment.

crossTeamPriorities.priority: Validate the alternate batch without changing NX-400 thresholds; track incidents separately.
crossTeamPriorities.dependency: Sample receipt, unchanged specifications, and independent sign-off.

crossTeamPriorities.priority: Keep resequencing conditional on confirmed material and labor readiness.
crossTeamPriorities.dependency: Released alternate material, interplant arrival, and workforce confirmation.

crossTeamPriorities.priority: Confirm inventory, customs routing, carrier capacity, and contingency routing before dispatch.
crossTeamPriorities.dependency: Finance authority, supplier commitment, and validated customs route.

crossTeamPriorities.priority: Confirm bounded overtime and two weekend shifts under labor policy.
crossTeamPriorities.dependency: Verified labor availability and production schedule.

crossTeamPriorities.priority: Prepare recovery-date drafts only; no external send is permitted.
crossTeamPriorities.dependency: Recalculated dates from confirmed logistics and manufacturing readiness.

crossTeamPriorities.priority: Maintain observation-versus-projection separation and assess Day 3, 7, 14, and 21 checkpoints.
crossTeamPriorities.dependency: Tool-confirmed evidence, all required approvals, and pause/re-escalation if Day 14 is below 93% or any hard constraint fails.

residualRisks: The compressor downside case reduces projected protection to 94.7%, below target.
Quality release, customs clearance, and weekend labor remain unconfirmed.
Only $34,000 of modeled budget headroom remains.
Medium-high confidence is simulated; final spend, deliveries, protection, and quality incidents remain unobserved.

findings.title: Required approvals remain pending
findings.detail: The pending Action Contract is expected, but all required approvals, particularly Global Procurement VP approval before ACT-004, must be documented before execution.

findings.title: Compressor downside misses the final target
findings.detail: The modeled downside reaches only 94.7%, while existing automatic re-escalation addresses Day 14 and hard-constraint failures rather than a forecast Day 21 target miss.

findings.title: Quality timing remains unverified
findings.detail: The alternate supplier requires fresh batch validation, and neither successful release nor completion within 36 hours is observed.

findings.title: Customs and carrier readiness remain conditional
findings.detail: Routing, reservation, and contingency guards are appropriate, but actual interplant clearance and carrier readiness remain unconfirmed.

findings.title: Weekend labor availability is unconfirmed
findings.detail: Production resequencing is safely dependency-gated, but availability for two weekend shifts and 960 overtime hours remains unsupported.

unsupportedClaims: Completion of alternate-batch quality release within 36 hours is not observed.
Customs clearance for the interplant transfer is not confirmed.
Weekend labor availability for two shifts and 960 overtime hours is not confirmed.
Compressor availability sufficient to preserve 95.0% Day 21 protection is unsupported; the modeled downside is 94.7%.
requiredConditions: Before execution, the Action Contract must document Plant COO, CFO-delegate, Global Procurement VP, and independent-audit clearance.
ACT-004 must remain paused until Quality records a passing BATCH-C17-R01 validation under unchanged NX-400 thresholds within the modeled 36-hour window.
ACT-005 must remain paused until inventory, carrier capacity, customs routing, and the contingency route are tool-confirmed at the Day 3 checkpoint.
ACT-008 must remain paused until Workforce Operations confirms policy-compliant availability for both weekend shifts and 960 overtime hours by Day 2.
Daily compressor monitoring must trigger immediate human re-escalation whenever the current Day 21 CRP projection falls below 95.0%.

counterfactual.scenario: The compressor constraint materializes while other Balanced Recovery assumptions hold.
counterfactual.expectedImpact: Projected Day 21 protection falls from 96.4% to 94.7%, missing the target by 0.3 percentage points and requiring human re-escalation.
